text: A 7-day taper with 85% reduction in training volume while maintaining high-intensity intervals improves 5-km running performance by approximately 3%.
specific_value: 3% improvement with 85% volume reduction over 7 days

text: Tapering with high-intensity intervals and reduced volume improves running economy, reducing submaximal oxygen consumption by approximately 6%.
Maintaining high-intensity work during a taper is important — replacing running intervals with equivalent cycling intervals does not improve running performance.
Cross-training with cycling during a taper period can maintain but not improve distance running performance compared to normal training.
A running taper reduces caloric expenditure at submaximal intensities by approximately 7%, indicating improved metabolic efficiency.
specific_value: 7% reduction in caloric expenditure at 80% VO2max

text: The specificity of training modality matters during taper — performance gains require sport-specific (running) intervals, not cross-training equivalents.
